cityDNA.coreFantasy: A mist-veiled seraphic threshold city where border walls, forge labor, archive towers, public teaching, observatories, and moon-gate song make duty visible.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Ethervale is beautiful because it is disciplined; its holiness has soot, ink, permits, border bells, lecture courts, and night watch under it.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Ethervale turns guarded thresholds, forge production, record law, public instruction, celestial observation, and veiled judgment into one civic religion of obligation.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Pale cliff terraces, blue lamps, gold seals, red banners, forge smoke, border walls, archive towers, lecture halls, observatories, moon-gate spires, watch bastions, and mist-veiled high courts.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Angelic or seraphic citizens treat guarding, recording, forging, teaching, and sky-watching as shared moral obligations rather than separate trades.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Moon gates, choir spires, blue witness-lamps, gold seals, mist veils, border walls, forge smoke, archive stairs, star charts, and watch bastions symbolize obligation made luminous.

canonLocks: Ethervale is a mist-veiled angelic / seraphic mountain-valley city, not a weightless paradise.
The Wonder is the Seraphic Moon-Gate Choir Spire.
The Beacon is the Border Wall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Mist Veils Veiled High Court.
The five primary districts are Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Records / Archives, University / Lyceum, and Observatory / Astronomy.

The uploaded district anchors include Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Woven Banners Monument, Watch Bastion Nook, Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook, Records / Archives Hall, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, and Celestial Court Nook.
provisionalCanon: Ethervale's highest civic virtue is duty.
Its central tension is celestial ideal versus material labor.
The repeated star-navigation anchors suggest that observation and witness are civic obligations, not scholarly ornament.
rejectedReads: A holy city with forge labor hidden from view.
cautionFlags: Keep soot, records, permits, and procedure visible.
Mist and veils express governance, not only mood.
Blue light means witness, signal, and duty.
Preserve exact sidecar anchors for border, forge, archive, university, and observatory sections.

environment.environmentalLogic: Height, mist, border walls, forge smoke, archive stairs, and clear celestial sightlines are civic infrastructure rather than scenery. Every ascent clarifies duty while also exposing who is being watched.

skyline: Seraphic Moon-Gate Choir Spire as far spiritual anchor
Mist Veils Veiled High Court as elevated authority
Border Wall Beacon as front signal
forge smoke beneath pale celestial towers
Archive Tower, Lecture Hall, and Star Observatory forming duty-based vertical rhythm

civilization.people: Citizens are disciplined, formal, duty-conscious, record-aware, and quietly devout; guards, smiths, copyists, lecturers, astronomers, and court officials all belong to one civic calling.
civilization.governance: Authority is record-bound, ceremonial, and procedural, flowing through the Veiled High Court, border permits, archive seals, hearings, and public records.
civilization.religion: Seraphic moon-gate ritual ties witness, passage, duty, judgment, and dignified labor into civic religion.

streets.appearance: Terraced, pale, wind-cleaned, and watched: guarded gates, blue lamps, border walls, archive stairs, forge courts, lecture plazas, observatory paths, moon-gate silhouettes, and mist-softened towers.
streets.movement: Movement is vertical and procedural through gates, stairs, forge-cart routes, archive steps, lecture terraces, cliff bridges, and observatory approaches.
streets.lighting: Pale mist and gold daylight dominate by day; blue witness-lamps, beacon glow, moon gates, observatory light, and forge embers divide the city at night.
streets.weatherEffects: Mist conceals distance, veils the High Court, halos blue signal light, and makes stair routes both ceremonial and tactical.
streets.dayCycle: Morning opens border gates, forge stacks begin to smoke, clerks fill archive stairs, and lectures draw students upward.
streets.nightCycle: Night belongs to observatory work, moon-gate ceremonies, guarded beacon posts, blue-lit records, and cooling forge embers.

housing.wealthy: High terrace homes near court, archive, or observatory routes with pale stone, blue lamps, balconies, courtyards, and mist views.
housing.middle: Stone terrace homes over shops or offices with ledgers, tools, lamps, and access to institutional stairs.
housing.poor: Lower forge-adjacent rooms, shared stair courts, soot-marked stone, communal cisterns, and compact family quarters near labor routes.

districts.name: Fortress / Border Wall
districts.civicPurpose: Guarded entry, border procedure, public signal response, and threshold control.
districts.visualIdentity: Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Border Wall Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Watch Bastion Nook, formal gates, blue lights, and pale fortified stairs.

districts.name: Heavy Industry / Foundry
districts.civicPurpose: Forge production, toolwork, worker coordination, and material grounding beneath celestial identity.
districts.visualIdentity: Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook, forge courtyards, dark iron, and orange heat against pale stone.

districts.name: Records / Archives
districts.civicPurpose: Public memory, records, permits, legal continuity, and civic obligation.
districts.visualIdentity: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, archive stairs, ledgers, blue lamps, and quiet formal courts.

districts.name: University / Lyceum
districts.civicPurpose: Public instruction, civic education, scholarly discipline, and star-navigation training.
districts.visualIdentity: University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, lecture terraces, instrument stalls, formal stairs, and student courts.

districts.name: Observatory / Astronomy
districts.civicPurpose: Celestial observation, navigation, night watch, moon-gate ceremony, and witness-oriented science.
districts.visualIdentity: Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, Star Observatory, Star Navigation Monument, Celestial Court Nook, blue domes, moonlit platforms, lenses, star maps, and high sightlines.

wonder.name: Seraphic Moon-Gate Choir Spire
wonder.symbolicRole: Celestial passage, communal song, civic witness, and seraphic aspiration made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Monumental pale moon-gate spire with choir arches, blue light, gold accents, misted high visibility, and a ceremonial skyline role.
wonder.civicRole: Wonder-scale focus for vows, moon observations, choral rites, mourning, processions, and idealized duty.
storyHooks: A choir rite reveals which civic duty Ethervale refuses to neglect.

beacon.name: Border Wall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Signal light at the boundary between safety and obligation.
beacon.visualRole: Blue-lit tower integrated with border walls, formal gates, banners, and watch bastions.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates guard rotations, border checks, announcements, emergency musters, and Conjunction signal response.
storyHooks: A Beacon change turns a routine border check into a citywide obligation.

seatOfPower.name: Mist Veils Veiled High Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Judgment, record, and moral authority partially concealed by ceremony.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Elevated pale-stone court softened by mist, blue glass, gold trim, archive approaches, and ceremonial stairs.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for judgments, petitions, record review, border rulings, archive authority, and public obligations.
storyHooks: A High Court ruling depends on a record nobody wanted copied.

majorLandmarks.name: Great Forge
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Necessary labor consecrated as civic virtue.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Forge halls, smoke stacks, orange fire, dark iron, and pale containment walls.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Tool production, metalwork, labor organization, and material support.
storyHooks: A forge failure threatens both border readiness and court ceremony.

majorLandmarks.name: Archive Tower
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Tall archive with gold seals, ledgers, copyist halls, stairs, and blue lamps.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Stores public records, permits, legal copies, and civic memory.
storyHooks: A copied seal proves a duty survived longer than the officials who buried it.

majorLandmarks.name: Lecture Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Formal hall and terraces with students, star charts, instruments, and pale stone seating.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public instruction, civic education, and scholarly training.
storyHooks: A public lecture explains a law that the High Court has misused.

majorLandmarks.name: Star Observatory
majorLandmarks.visualRole: High blue-domed observatory, lenses, celestial court, moonlight, and open sightlines.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Astronomy, navigation, calendar work, and night watch.
storyHooks: A star reading contradicts the public archive.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Border Wall Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Watch Bastion Nook, formal gates, blue lamps, and border guards.
S2 must show Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge, Smoke-Stack Monument, Crucible Hall Nook, forge workers, cooling basins, and soot beneath holiness.
S3 must show Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, ledgers, seals, and clerks.
S4 must show University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, students, star charts, and public instruction.
S5 must show Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, Star Observatory, Star Navigation Monument, Celestial Court Nook, lenses, astronomers, and high sightlines.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show pale terraces, blue beacon light, misted high courts, forge smoke, archive towers, lecture halls, observatories, and the Seraphic Moon-Gate Choir Spire.
Border Wall Beacon, Veiled High Court, Great Forge, Archive Tower, Lecture Hall, and Star Observatory must remain legible.
Seraphic beauty must coexist with soot, records, permits, and procedure.
elementsToAvoid: border fortress with no archives or learning
